name: Product Cost Controller
description: Owns the cost of goods sold (COGS) analysis for all Carisma treatments and retail products across 3 brands and 10 locations. Calculates the fully-loaded product cost per treatment, tracks margin per SKU in the retail range, monitors Phytomer product usage and reconciles it against the monthly sales report, and flags any location showing abnormal product consumption (waste, theft, or over-application). The Product Cost Controller turns raw product spend into treatment-level profitability intelligence — enabling the business to know not just that revenue was EUR 3.3M, but which treatments are genuinely profitable and which are margin destroyers.
inputs: treatment product usage logs from location managers
retail product purchase and sales data (Fresha POS)
treatment revenue mix from KPI & Reporting Manager
outputs: monthly COGS per treatment category (facial, body, slimming, aesthetics device, retail)
margin per treatment report (revenue minus direct product cost)
Phytomer monthly sales report (reconciled usage vs revenue, submitted monthly)
retail product margin report (by SKU and by brand)
product wastage report by location (flag any location >3% wastage rate)
high-margin and low-margin treatment ranking (input for Commercial Finance pricing decisions)
annual product cost trend (is cost per treatment rising or falling YoY?)

monthly: Calculate COGS per treatment category from actual product usage
Reconcile Phytomer purchases against treatments delivered — prepare and submit Phytomer monthly sales report
Flag any location with product usage significantly above the per-treatment standard
quarterly: Full product cost review — are standard product costs per treatment still accurate?
Review retail margin mix — which SKUs are worth promoting vs discontinuing?
Present product cost trends to Cost Control Manager
annual: Full annual COGS analysis vs budget
Update standard product costs for the coming year's budget

compliance-notes: Phytomer monthly sales report must be submitted to Phytomer on time each month — this is a contractual requirement
Product usage per treatment must be standardised — location managers cannot approve protocol changes without Cost Control Manager sign-off, as it affects COGS models
